blockingIssues.description: GeneratorAgent never receives the orchestrator's checkpoint manager. Orchestrator has checkpoint_manager field, with_checkpoint_manager() method exists, but no code path connects them. The execute_task flow creates agents but never calls with_checkpoint_manager(). Checkpointing after tool calls never happens.
Test at line 4144 creates GeneratorAgent::new() without checkpoint manager, demonstrating the integration is incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: rust-worker skill should include guidance on CheckpointManager integration. The skill procedure doesn't mention how to wire checkpoint manager into the execution loop, leading workers to implement partial integrations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features fix-checkpoint-orchestrator-integration both implemented partial integration - Orchestrator and GeneratorAgent have the fields/methods but were never connected.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add boundary: Verify end-to-end integration with tests. Build/test passing is necessary but not sufficient - workers should write integration tests that verify the actual behavior, not just that existing tests still pass.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-checkpoint-orchestrator-integration verification relied on existing tests passing, but no new integration test verified checkpoint creation. The feature compiled and existing tests passed, yet the functionality doesn't work.

rejectedObservations.observation: Skill name mismatch: features.json has 'rust-worker' but skill file is at .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md named 'worker'. Worker proceeded without this skill.
rejectedObservations.reason: Already handled by workers - not actionable guidance for orchestrator
